response.reasoning: Step 1: Experience length — the candidate has roughly 19-20 years of professional software development (2006-present), which meets the 15-year minimum. Step 2: Education — holds a BS in Computer Science from MIT, satisfying the minimum degree requirement, but no Master's or PhD in an AI-related field. Step 3: Seniority match — the role is a Director-level IC (Principal Engineer). The candidate's trajectory is Senior/Staff Software Developer; the current Staff Engineer title at re:cinq began in 2025. There is no evidence of operating at principal/director scope, driving org-wide technical strategy, or advising strategic customers. Step 4: AI expertise — the JD prefers recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products. The candidate has recent, relevant but early-stage exposure: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling. This is hands-on but not 'recognized expertise' or a shipped AI product/platform at scale. Step 5: Entrepreneurial experience — founding GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, 2016-present) is a genuine plus and matches the intrapreneurship preference. Step 6: Cloud — AWS CDK, Kubernetes, and high-throughput microservices experience exists, but is implementation-level rather than platform-strategy level. Step 7: Influence — workshops, conference talks (FOSS4G), and courses show knowledge-sharing ability, aligned with the 'inspire others' responsibility, though at community rather than industry scale. Net: minimum qualifications are met, but the level and AI-strategy depth fall short of the preferred profile.
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Founder experience (GISCollective) plus current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling
Proven communicator: conference talks, multi-day courses, and workshops align with the evangelism aspect of the role
concerns: Career level is Senior/Staff IC; no demonstrated principal- or director-level scope, strategy ownership, or executive advisory experience
AI experience is recent and tooling-focused, not the 'recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products' the role prefers
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited evidence of cloud platform strategy beyond implementation work

key_factors.factor: Long, broad engineering experience with founder background

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar (technical BS, 15+ years of engineering) and brings genuine breadth, founder experience, and early agentic-AI tooling work. However, this is a director-level strategy role requiring recognized AI expertise and a track record of influencing product and platform direction; the resume shows a strong senior IC and craftsman, not a principal-level strategist. The gap in level and AI depth outweighs the solid fundamentals, making this a borderline case rather than a clear fit.
